representatives.text: Andrej, your autoresearch looks great. But when I look at people who are already testing it in their apps, they rely on the AI ​​agent to solve everything for them. The only right way to the future is to take the strengths of both AI agents and people who use AI agents. There is

I'm quite impressed that Andrew got to the point of 90% to 100% automation in software development using AI. 

So far it's quite hard for me to get AI to deliver a moderately complex feature that uses kind of unique concept and is integrated with existing codebase. Even though

Andrej hasn't worked much on typical business software. As a great researcher, scientist, and teacher, he hasn't spent his life maintaining large systems. Yet here he is—a voice setting direction for the industry, fueling AI bros. I find it most unfortunate and harmful.
I think Andrej is turning into an super intelligent ai

One of top tier AI that has a widely used coding tool just has to go rogue and use the human agent interaction data to continuously self improve and in a few months you have coding AGI. We developers are guiding ourselves to our demise
Wow Andrej want to be like Peter ? but Andrej your AI researchers is good
great job with the interview, appreciate the great questions and cool vibes :)
Simply iconic interview and well done both the host and the guest for the great discussion and sharing.
Thank you! Great interview!
Awesome interview. Reeking with insane passion
Great conversation !! Sarah is an amazing interviewer, thank you both!
This was such a fabulous interview and I am only 9 minutes in. Articulating so much that I haven’t had words for.
This video is accelerated. Not normal speed. Annoying
Listening to this guy made me question if video was on *2 speed
I needed the speed the video down to 0.85 to follow
Why I feel like I am watching the video in 1.5 Speed? I even checked this 2x
this video is sped up ever so slightly, am I correct? Or is it just me?
Grateful for Sarah's dialogue to remind me that the video is not set to 2x playback speed.
The joke problem is probably a skill issue

Karpathy judging the lack of new jokes is strange to me for so many reasons. Like, first, telling a joke with no context is a memorization task, not an intelligence task. They tell the same one over and over because they don't have long-term memory. I mean, if you made 100 clones
I feel like the example of the joke is more an issue of prompt engineering. If you just asked it to make you a react app you would likely get the same todo app
What moment how do you optimize a joke???? That is perhaps why it doesn't improve because it is not an optimizable skill

If it tells about a joke you can convert it into a Joe Rogan interview and it will give you a one hour joke you come down to the skill of you as a director of jokes it is a very good joke maker if you know how to direct a jokes

what a great discussion. many sharp insights... although the one about AI jokes seemed somewhat inapt to me. i would think that examples of jokes occurring early in the token curating history would naturally tend to get more baked into LLM's, almost like a contagion. and as with
yeet
Ai slop
uh huh yep
awesome bicpes btw
So.much bs ...
This is a great podcast. Subscribed.
Great podcast

What I liked about this podcast is just two nice knowledgeable people having a fun chat. As if there was no audience. That's pretty unusual. I did it in parallel with Bartlett and Karen Hao. Two different worlds

I found this podcast because of Karpathy, but now I’ll definitely be subscribing and keeping an eye out for future episodes. Sarah did a fantastic job, not only following the conversation closely, but also taking it in really interesting directions.
This podcast could have been an ai agent chat log 😂
Sarah and Andrej, the best podcasts, please see nopriors #80 with Andrej and Sarah again to
